items.title: Australia's most remote communities losing essential services due to price of fuel

children.content: Cultural obligations and essential services are being impacted by the cost of fuel in Australia's most remote communities.

items.title: Firefighters who died battling blaze, from cancer added to honour roll

children.content: Hundreds of people have honoured the families and legacies of Western Australia's fallen firefighters at a memorial service in Kings Park on Sunday.

image.altText: A memorial service is held at the Firefighter's Memorial Grove in Kings Park.

items.title: Emerging Indigenous artists reveal their stories in major showcase

children.content: Revealed, Perth's long-running showcase of Aboriginal art from around Western Australia, allows budding artists to discover their creative side while strengthening their cultural bonds.

image.altText: Woven sculptures of cars displayed on a wall

items.title: Expert slams WA as last state to close child safety loophole

children.content: Western Australia is the only state to not recognise Working with Children Check rejections from around the country, and has been "slow" to make other child safety law changes, expert says.

gallery.altText: Two people hand over a Working with Children Check card against a blue background.

A child plays with puzzles at a table.

items.title: Man, 18, dies after car rolls over on Perth's Mitchell Freeway

children.content: The man, who was the sole occupant of the vehicle, sustained critical injuries and died a short time later after being conveyed to Royal Perth Hospital.

image.altText: A long shot shows heavy traffic on the Mitchell Freeway.

items.title: Untold stories of women in whaling inspire art that delves into the past

children.content: The overlooked contributions of women in the whaling industry are the subject of an exhibition in the Great Southern region of Western Australia.

items.title: Farmers say levees built to prevent flooding have made situation worse

children.content: In Western Australia's biggest food bowl region, farmers are still assessing the damage from the latest flood caused by Cyclone Narelle. However, farmers say the levees built to reduce damage made the situation worse.

items.title: 'Friendships ended': Jewish Australians reveal impact of antisemitism

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will hear from people with lived experience in public sessions beginning in Sydney this week.

items.title: Japan's PM touches down in Canberra for first visit to Australia

children.content: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi has touched down in Canberra for her first visit to Australia. Follow live.

items.title: What's turning this red-tailed black cockatoo orange?

children.content: Spotted again in WA's South West, a rare condition is the reason for the bird's distinctive orange colouring.

items.title: We're headed into an AI fog as the automation arms race begins

children.content: Artificial intelligence might be driving growth for data centre companies, but society is unprepared for the long-term risks posed to the rest of us.

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

image.altText: Bronte looks down at the camera as she paints

items.title: Interest rates and recession: What to know before tomorrow's RBA decision

children.content: Business journalist Emily Stewart walks you through the elements that will contribute to the RBA's decision to change the cash rate on Tuesday.

items.title: Police charge Jefferson Lewis, 47, with 5yo girl's murder

children.content: Northern Territory Police have charged Jefferson Lewis with the murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs.

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

items.title: Federal government to back 10 renewable energy projects in regional WA

children.content: WA's energy minister says the state will have the fuel it needs for this month, as further details are released about 10 renewable energy projects that are set to be built across regional WA.

items.title: Contaminants are in your clothes, and brands don't need to tell you

children.content: Despite the rising flood of fast fashion coming into Australia, there's little regulation on hazardous substances in clothes.

image.altText: Dozens of brightly coloured T-shirts hanging off coathangers in a clothing store.

items.title: What can you use your personal leave for?

children.content: People employed on a permanent basis in Australia are entitled to 10 days' paid personal leave a year to help navigate illness, caring responsibilities, or family emergencies. But what are the different types of personal leave and when can you use them?

image.altText: A worried-looking mother with a thermometer in her hand is on the phone, while a child lays down on the couch beside her.

children.content: Take a trip back in time with this week's history quiz.

image.altText: A shot of the $50 Australian note with a thumb holding it.

items.title: As fuel costs soar, cycling advocates argue our priorities are all wrong

children.content: Bike shops in Darwin are experiencing soaring demand as more people turn to one of the oldest and simplest alternatives to cars amid skyrocketing fuel prices, but advocates say the federal government is still prioritising drivers.

image.altText: A man smiles in a bike workshop.

items.title: Ghost rainbows, storms and sunsets — WA's skies, captured by you

children.content: In classic shoulder season conditions, April delivered a transitional mix of warm beach weather and cool, wet days with rainfall in the double-digits as we inch closer to winter.

items.title: Piastri makes late charge for Miami GP podium as teen makes more F1 history

children.content: Oscar Piastri says the future is exciting for McLaren after the Australian made a late charge to finish third in the Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix.

image.altText: Third placed Oscar Piastri of Australia and McLaren lifts his trophy on the podium

items.title: News avoidance on the rise as young people feel afraid and angry

children.content: The news has never been more accessible, yet people are tuning out at record levels. Here is how to strike a balance between keeping informed and looking after yourself.

items.title: Dingo sightings near primary school put parents on edge

children.content: "Stressed-out" parents are wondering whether to let their children continue to get around unaccompanied, but wildlife advocates say the animals are not as threatening as their reputation suggests.

items.title: Work underway to bring plants to life after fire ravaged Stirling Range

children.content: Seed collections taken before major bushfires in WA's Stirling Range National Park give hope for the continued survival of flora species found nowhere else in the world.

image.altText: A woman standing on a mountaintop surrounded by plants

items.title: Australian taxpayers take on risk for major oil and gas projects

children.content: Australian governments take on risk to get major resource projects up and running, so why don't they take an equity stake?
